description:
Large counter (~10 KB of source) — emits a '0
1
2
…' sequence with no stdin.

2017-addison-bean.notes: Panics with `already borrowed: BorrowMutError` on the first FishDown tick — river.rs:237's move_salmon borrows the parent mutably while the tick recursion still holds it, so any multi-level tree crashes before producing output.

2018-martijn-arts.notes: Parser splits on spaces only, so the newline + indent structure of the source collapses into a flat tree — the intended river layout is lost and output is either empty or a stream of literal node-name salmon.

2023-james-thistlewood.notes:
Produces no output in 80 ticks — the reference interpreters emit '0
1
2
' well within that, so James's per-tick state transitions diverge from the spec on this program.
